page.title: Correlation rules
page.description: Define cross-source threshold rules to detect anomalous patterns across multiple events. Rules are evaluated at the end of each federated search, over its results.

betaNotice.title: Beta — evaluation is not continuous
betaNotice.body: An enabled rule is only evaluated when a federated search finishes, and only over that search's results. The ingestion pipeline does not evaluate rules and there is no scheduling of its own: as long as no one runs a federated search, no correlation detection is created. Do not use these rules as your only detection coverage.

feedback.toggleDisabled: Rule "{{name}}" disabled — it stops generating new detections within 1 collection cycle.

createCard.title: New rule
createCard.description: Configure the correlation criteria applied to the results of each completed federated search.

catalogCard.title: Rule catalog
catalogCard.description: Manage the correlation rules enabled on the platform. Enabled rules join the next federated search.

search.placeholder: Search by name, description or group-by field…

toggle.tooltipInflight: Stops generating new detections within 1 collection cycle. Does not remove detections already created.
toggle.tooltipDefault: Stops being evaluated on the next federated search.

empty.title: No correlation rules
empty.description: Create rules to detect cross-source patterns and automatically generate detections.

deleteDialog.title: Remove correlation rule
deleteDialog.description: Delete the rule {{name}}? Future detections generated by it will stop.

timestamp.label: Timestamp field
timestamp.helperText: Event field used to apply the window. Without it, the window is turned off and the rule counts every event in the group across the search results.

validation.timestampRequired: Required when there is a window — without it the window is ignored

description.placeholder: Context and purpose of the rule

groupBy.helperText: Event field used to group occurrences within the window.

whereFilter.heading: Filters (where)
whereFilter.empty: No filter added — the rule will evaluate every event.

preview.heading: Test against samples
preview.description: Runs this rule against a real sample of events already received for the chosen vendor and event type — nothing is persisted. Each test opens a new backend query; it never runs automatically.

vendor.label: Vendor
vendor.helperText: Required — the sample is indexed by vendor + event type.
vendor.loadError: Could not load active integrations — enter the vendor manually.

eventType.label: Event type
eventType.helperText: Required — the sample is indexed by vendor + event type.
eventType.emptyForVendor: No event type cataloged for this vendor — enter it manually.

disabledReason.noWhere: Add at least one filter (where) to test.
disabledReason.noVendorOrEventType: Choose vendor and event type to test.

freshness.unknown: Could not determine the samples' time window.
freshness.staleWarning: The most recent sample is more than {{days}} day(s) old. The reservoir never expires on its own — this may be a stalled integration, not live traffic.

summary.matched: {{matched}} of {{total}} sample(s) matched the full rule

empty.title: No samples
empty.description: There are no sampled events yet for this vendor + event type. Confirm the integration is active and sending data, or try again shortly.

unavailable.title: Telemetry unavailable
unavailable.description: Could not query the sample reservoir right now — the telemetry backend is down. This does NOT mean the rule wouldn't match: try again.

invalid.title: Invalid rule
invalid.description: This rule does not compile and would never be evaluated.

reason.bad_json: The filter (where) has an invalid shape — review the field, operator and value of each row.
reason.empty_where: The rule has no filter (where) — add at least one before testing.
reason.unknown_op: One of the operators used isn't supported by the correlation engine.
reason.over_cap: The rule has more filters than the correlation engine supports — reduce the number of clauses.

requestError.title: Failed to test the rule
requestError.description: Could not complete the test call.

clauses.heading: Verdict by clause
clauses.pathNotFound: Field "{{path}}" was not found in any sample — the path may be wrong.
clauses.pathFoundNoMatch: Field exists in {{resolved}} of {{total}} sample(s), but the value doesn't match the operator.
